title: Numeric tower R7RS-correctness edge cases (sqrt-neg complex, max/min contagion, exact expt, inf display)
goal: Found by ESH-0052 numeric-tower edge probing. Four R7RS-correctness gaps (Eshkol already has the substrate to fix them): (1) (sqrt -4) returns nan, but R7RS 6.2.6 says a negative real sqrt returns a COMPLEX (+2i) — Eshkol has complex numbers, so sqrt must promote negatives to complex. (2) (max 1 2.0) returns exact 2, but R7RS requires inexactness CONTAGION: if any argument is inexact the result is inexact -> 2.0; same for min. (3) (expt 2 -2) returns inexact 0.25, but exact base + negative-exact exponent should give an exact rational 1/4. (4) (/ 1.0 0.0) prints `inf`; the R7RS external representation is `+inf.0` (and -inf.0 / +nan.0). Confirmed CORRECT already: int64->bignum overflow, expt bignum, modulo/remainder signs, banker's rounding, rational reduction, bignum bitwise, string->number rationals.
next_actions: sqrt: when the operand is a negative real, return a complex result (sqrt(-x) = (* (sqrt x) i)) instead of nan.
max/min: apply inexactness contagion — if any argument is inexact, coerce the (exact-valued) result to inexact.
expt: exact base with negative exact integer exponent -> exact rational (1/base^|n|), not a double.
Number->string / display: emit +inf.0 / -inf.0 / +nan.0 for the infinities and NaN (R7RS external representation).
Add tests/numeric/numeric_tower_edge_test.esk covering all four + the already-correct cases as regression guards.
acceptance: all previously-correct numeric cases still pass; AOT + -r
campaign_payoff: Tightens R7RS numeric-tower conformance (sqrt-of-negative, inexactness contagion, exact expt, infinity/NaN printing) — all needed for ALL of SICP + general R7RS correctness.
